questions.question: What is a schema in the context of database management systems?
options: A) A set of database objects
B) A set of data structures
C) A set of rules governing data
D) A set of data types
questions.explanation: A schema is a conceptual representation of the structure of a database, including the relationships between different data entities.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a schema in database management systems?
options: A) To store data
B) To manage data
C) To define the structure of the database
D) To perform queries
questions.explanation: The primary purpose of a schema is to define the structure of the database, including the relationships between different data entities.

questions.question: What is an example of a schema in a database management system?
options: A) A table with columns and rows
B) A query with conditions and filters
C) A view with selected data
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: A table with columns and rows is an example of a schema in a database management system.

questions.question: What happens when a database schema is modified?
options: A) The data remains the same
B) The data is updated
C) The data is deleted
D) The database is recreated
questions.explanation: When a database schema is modified, the data is updated to reflect the changes in the schema.

questions.question: Why is it important to have a well-designed schema?
options: A) To improve data security
B) To increase data storage
C) To improve data retrieval and manipulation
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: A well-designed schema is important to improve data retrieval and manipulation, as it ensures that the data is organized and structured in a way that makes it easy to access and manipulate.
